calendar.matters:
Petition For Instructions And Orders: 1) For Suspension Of David Bartels Powers As Trustee And Appointment Of Temporary Trustee To Serve Pending Resolution Of His Trust Construction Petition; Andor 2) For David Bartels Removal As Trustee And Appointment Of A Successor Trustee; 3) Compelling David Bartel To Return To The Trust Funds Wrongfully Distributed In 2023, And Surcharging Him For Breaches Of Trust; 4) Bringing The Trust Under Court Supervision And Prohibiting David Bartel From Paying His Attorney'S Fees With Trust Funds; And 5) For The Posting Of A Bond; 6) Affirming That The Trustee'S
Off Calendar Jun-12-2024 Off Calendar

Petition For Instructions, Specifically For Order: 1) Approving Settlement Agreement; 2) Reserving Jurisdiction To Enforce Settlement Agreement Under Ccp 664.6; And 3) For Such Other Relief As The Probate Court Deems Just And Proper Pursuant To Its Equitable Powers
Off Calendar Jun-18-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-23-306000 The Peggy Bartel Living Trust and
PTR-24-307150 The Harry Glassman Trust.

Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for and with Verna Shaheen.
Ciaran O'Sullivan for Alley Cat Allies.
Tyson Hubbard appeared by zoom for David Bartel (trustee of the Bartel trust).
Stacie Nelson appeared by zoom for 9 of the charitable beneficiaries of the Glassman Trust.
Kevin Rodriguez appeared by zoom for the Oakland Zoo.
Phil Bond appeared by zoom for the Zoological Society of San Diego (beneficiary of the Glassman Trust).
Keith Race (beneficiary of the Glassman Trust) in propria persona.

Mrs. Calhoun informed the court that there is a typo in the proposed orders Page 5, paragraph, 7. e. (Trust "o" after" should read "on or after") and asked the court to please correct it, addressed the issues raised in the probate examiner's notes, and read and explained the terms of the settlement agreement. The Petitions for Instructions Specifically for Order Approving Settlement Agreement, etc. in both cases are under submission. The Petition for Instructions and Orders for Suspension in the Bartel Trust is off calendar.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

Petition For Instructions, Specifically For Order: 1) Approving Settlement Agreement; 2) Reserving Jurisdiction To Enforce Settlement Agreement Under Ccp 664.6; And 3) For Such Other Relief As The Probate Court Deems Just And Proper Pursuant To Its Equitable Powers
Off Calendar Jun-11-2024 Continued To Jun-12-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Off Calendar Aug-24-2023 Off Calendar

Petition For Orders Construing Instrument And Instructing Trustee To Distribute Trust Assets Pursuan
Off Calendar Jun-28-2023 Continued To Oct-02-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dustin Amrein, Esq., counsel for and with David Bartel, Petitioner; Stacie Nelson, Esq., counsel for various charitable trust beneficiaries; Ciaran O'Sullivan, Esq., counsel for Alley Cats Allies (charitable trust beneficiary) (all appearances by video conference); Emily Calhoun, Esq., counsel for and with Verna Shaheen, Trustee [related trust](present in the courtroom).

The Court commences the hearing by inquiring as to whether the parties were amenable to mediation and whether a Breslin Order may be appropriate. Ms. Calhoun contends that the Court's proposition is premature in that she contends venue is not proper in San Francisco since the trust is administered in Sacramento County and the Trustee resides in Arizona; that not all inchoate beneficiaries received notice of the proceedings; and finally, that the trial preference motion was untimely and the resulting Order sending the matter to Dept. 206 for trial should be vacated.

Court and counsel discuss the venue issue with Mr. O'Sullivan arguing for venue change along with Ms. Calhoun. Mr. Amrein contends that venue remains proper in San Francisco for reasons stated on the record.

Court and counsel discuss the appropriate Court authority for hearing the Motion for Change of Venue: whether it should remain in the Probate Department or in Dept. 302 - Law & Motion, since the matter has been referred to Dept. 206 - Master Calendar - for trial.

Returning to mediation, most counsel are amenable to mediation although Mr. O'Sullivan is of the opinion that the proper venue should first be determined. After discussion, the Court will prepare a Breslin Order sending the matter to mediation.

[Post Script: after hearing and further consultation, the Court determines that the Motion for Change will be heard in Dept. 204.]
Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Choi. Reporter: Tina Marie Velasquez CSR #10072, 510-301-2918, velasqueztina@gmail.com. (D204)

The parties presented oral argument to support their positions. Upon conclusion of argument, the Motion for Preference in Setting the Case for Trial is under submission.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported.
